name: Binance

evidence.claim: Binance provides official documentation for its Spot APIs and streams, including REST and WebSocket interfaces.
Developers generate API keys via the API Management section in their account settings to access private trading and account endpoints.
Public endpoints are accessible without authentication, while private endpoints require API key and signature verification.

access_notes: Developers can generate API keys directly through the API Management section of their personal Binance account settings.
mcp_notes: No official or prominent community-maintained Model Context Protocol (MCP) server was identified in the official documentation.
auth_notes: Private endpoints require an API key and an HMAC SHA256 signature (the API also supports RSA and Ed25519 keys). Public endpoints can be accessed without authentication.
one_liner: Binance provides a comprehensive API that allows developers to programmatically access market data, manage accounts, and execute cryptocurrency trades.
api_notes: The API includes comprehensive support for market data, trading (Spot), user data, and WebSocket streams. It also supports FIX protocol.
